jobs.description: I am seeking a highly technical Shopify Developer to perform a deep-clean and genuine performance optimization of my one-product store. I think there are speed cloaking scripts in my Shopify store, which are giving a fake high score in PageSpeed Insights & GTmetrix. The site uses GemPages for the main product page, which features one video and several high-resolution images. Crucially, the site has Server-Side Tracking (SST) and Enhanced Conversion tracking active. My goal is to completely remove the "fake" performance layer and replace it with legitimate, white-hat optimizations without interfering with any existing tracking or data layers. Project Scope: 1. Forensic Removal: Locate and strip all cloaking scripts from all possible locations, including but not limited to theme.liquid, snippets, assets, GemPages custom code/scripts, and the Shopify ScriptTag API. The goal is a 100% clean environment. 2. Tracking Integrity: All optimizations must be implemented without interfering with existing GTM/Server-Side Tracking. No tracking pixels or conversion triggers should be deferred or blocked. 3. GemPages & Product Page Optimization: Legitimate optimization of the GemPages landing page and the default Shopify product template. 4. Media Handling: Implement proper facades for the video and convert images to WebP/AVIF with correct fetch priorities. 5. Restoration: Ensure the removal of hacks does not break GemPages, the cart, or the checkout flow. All work must be performed on a duplicate theme first. Milestones & Payment: Milestone 1: Discovery, Audit & Clean Baseline ($60) • Scan all theme files, GemPages custom code, and content_for_header for bot-detection logic. • Deliverable: A list of affected files and a transparent performance baseline with cloaking disabled. Milestone 2: Removal, Restoration & Real Optimization ($230) • Manually extract cloaking code on a duplicate theme. • Tracking Safety Check: Use GTM Preview mode to confirm that existing tracking tags (SST/Enhanced Conversions) continues to trigger as they did prior to optimization. The freelancer is responsible for ensuring optimization scripts do not block or delay these pre-existing triggers. • Optimize GemPages assets and fix Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS) without black-hat blocking. Milestone 3: Verification and Monitoring ($60) • 48-hour observation window to ensure "ghost scripts" do not re-inject. • Final Audit: Payment is subject to a technical review confirming zero bot-detection logic and proof that GTM is still passing hashed customer data correctly. Requirements: • Expert-level Shopify Liquid and Advanced JavaScript. • Specific experience with GemPages and its custom code limitations. • Proven experience removing "fake speed" scripts (mention this in your proposal). • Deep understanding of Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, INP).
jobs.title: Shopify Forensic Audit: Remove Speed Cloaking + Real Optimization (GemPages / One-Product Store)

Scope of Work We want to create a bundle product page that includes: --- 1. Bundle Structure * Bundle consists of multiple products (e.g. mattress + bed frame) * Backend SKUs must remain tied to individual products * Orders should reflect correct individual items (not just a single bundle SKU) --- 2. Variant & Size Syncing * Products have variants (e.g. sizes: Single, Queen, King) * When a user selects a size, it must apply to all products in the bundle simultaneously * Variant pricing must update dynamically based on selection --- 3. Pricing Logic (Critical) * Bundle price must be pulled dynamically from individual product prices based on selected variants * Apply a bundle discount (%) * Display: * original combined price * discounted bundle price * savings amount / percentage * No hardcoding — pricing must stay in sync if product prices change --- 4. UI / UX Requirements * Display mini product blocks within the bundle: * thumbnail * product name * key info * Each mini product should open a lightbox/modal with more details * Clean, premium UI (similar to Koala reference) * Must integrate well with Flatsome theme --- **5. Add to Cart & Checkout (Critical)** * Add-to-cart must correctly add all bundle items with correct variants * Cart must reflect accurate pricing and not conflict with existing WooCommerce logic * Checkout must preserve correct itemisation and totals * No duplication, mismatch, or pricing errors --- **6. Compatibility & Stability** * Must work with existing WooCommerce setup and plugins * No breaking of: * current product pages * cart behaviour * checkout flow --- **Technical Expectations** * WooCommerce hooks & filters * Custom pricing logic (e.g. before_calculate_totals) * AJAX / dynamic updates * Custom plugin or extendable architecture * PHP, JS, WooCommerce internals Bonus: * Experience with bundle/upsell systems * Shopify/Liquid experience --- **What We’re Looking For** * Someone who can handle both: * frontend implementation (replicating UI cleanly) * backend logic (pricing, cart, variants) * Strong problem-solver (not just plugin installer) * Attention to detail — pricing accuracy is critical --- **To Apply, Please Include** 1. Relevant WooCommerce work (especially bundles, cart, or pricing logic) 2. Examples of: * custom product pages * upsell/bundle implementations 3. Brief explanation of: * how you would approach dynamic bundle pricing + variant syncing --- We are looking for someone we can continue working with for future ecommerce enhancements.

jobs.description: We need to migrate a multi-country/multilingual website ae.scotch-soda.com and sa.schotch-soda.com from magento to shopify. There will be two countries UAE, and Saudi Arabia, and two languages English and Arabic per country. We will use Impulse Shopify Team for the frontend Price and Stock File Imports: A stock file and price will be uploaded to an FTP daily. We need a cron to read the files and update the price and stock once daily in the morning. Data Migration: Products, Orders, Customers data migration will be handled by another vendor. Payment Options: 4 payment options Checkout - Shopify plugin available and needs configuration Tabby - Shopify plugin available and needs configuration Tamara - Shopify plugin available and needs configuration Cash on Delivery - Additional AED/SAR 15 surcharge needs to be charged for cash on delivery orders. Shipping: Free shipping on all orders except for orders from outlet category. Baskets having all items from outlet category need to be charged AED/SAR 15 shipping fee. Click and Collect from Store: Customers can choose to pickup their order from stores. Real-Time Stock check: On magento we have a module that does a real time stock check by calling our ERP via API endpoint. The API is called when the cart page is loaded. It returns the stock of the items in the basket, and if any item is out of stock, it gets removed from the basket. We need to re-use the same module from magento for shopify Promotions: These promotions need to be setup Always-On: 15% discount on All Non-discounted product using a coupon code Seasonal- Buy 2 get the lowest priced item free BUY 2 GET EXTRA 10% OFF, BUY 3 GET EXTRA 20% OFF & BUY 4 GET EXTRA 30% OFF Buy More Save More - Spend 1000 get 200 off or Spend 2000 get 400 off Theme: We will configure the theme pages - homepage, PLP, PDP, design, colours typography etc. We need assistance with the following: Designing the mega menu with imagesFixing the CSS for any RTL issues on the arabic pages Marketing: Product Feed order export Member Sale days: During member sale days, we need to hide products from the main categories and display them only in SALE categories. We had a feature in magento built to do this because it takes a lot of time to remove products. But in shopify if we can remove products in categories in bulk and then add them back again after few days by csv import, we dont need this feature.
jobs.title: Migration From Magento to Shopify

jobs.description: We're building a client portal that tracks program allocations, usage, and remaining balances. Before we can build it, we need to standardize our invoicing in Shopify — right now there's no clean separation between what's sold and what's fulfilled, which means there's no reliable data structure to map from. We need someone who can help us define that structure and build the invoice layer on top of it. What you'll do Advise on and define a program-level data schema in Shopify (program name, tier, unit allocation, rate per home, shipping, coverage period, total value) Set up the Shopify structure using metafields or structured line items — whichever best supports downstream data mapping Build a custom Order Printer invoice template that matches our visual mockup — clean, elevated, brand-aligned Ensure the invoice structure is consistent and reliable enough to serve as a data source for a future Airtable/portal integration Longer-term context Once this is in place, Shopify will be the source of truth for billing, and Airtable/the portal will track inventory allocated, usage, and remaining balance. We're sharing this context upfront so you can make informed recommendations on structure. What we're looking for Solid experience with Shopify metafields, Order Printer, and Liquid templating Comfortable thinking about data structure, not just visual design Detail-oriented with clean, well-commented code Nice to have Experience with Airtable — structuring bases, views, and relational data Familiarity with automation tools (Make, Zapier, or native Airtable automations) to connect Shopify and Airtable Experience building client portals with Softr — this is the direction we're heading for the portal layer We have a visual mockup ready to share. Please include examples of past Shopify invoice or template work in your proposal, and briefly describe how you'd approach structuring program-level data in Shopify. Bonus points if you have Airtable, automation, or Softr projects to show.
jobs.title: Shopify invoice template + data structure for client portal

jobs.description: Overview I run a Shopify store with high‑AOV products and rely on Google Ads and Meta Ads. Tracking has been unreliable since a while. We need a true **expert** to audit, rebuild, and validate tracking across GTM, GA4, Google Ads, and Meta. Goals You will: - Audit and fix tracking for: - GTM - GA4 - Google Ads conversions & audiences - Meta pixel & Conversions API - Implement a clean event setup and ensure accurate reporting from click to purchase. - Document the setup so we can maintain and extend it. Events and Data to Track We want, at minimum: - Page view - View item / product view - View item list / collection view (if relevant) - Add to cart - Begin checkout - Add payment info (if available) - Purchase (with transaction ID, value, currency, items) - WhatsApp button click - Live chat button click - Book appointment / schedule call click For these events, send key parameters (e.g., product ID/SKU, value, currency, quantity, page URL, button location). You can propose additions based on best practice. Additionally: - Help‑me‑choose funnel events: - Form view (widget or section visible) - Form start (first interaction / first question answered) - Form step progress (optional but nice: step 1, step 2, step 3…) - Form submit / completed - Recommendation shown / recommendation selected We want a clear funnel view for this “help me choose” flow: how many users see it, start it, complete it, and select a recommendation, so we can see drop‑offs and performance by traffic source and device Scope 1) Audit - Review Shopify, GTM, GA4, Google Ads, Meta pixel/CAPI, and any tracking apps. - Identify broken, duplicate, or missing events, plus data mismatches between platforms. - Deliver a short audit with prioritized fixes. 2) Tracking plan - Define final event list and naming per platform. - Specify parameters and mapping: Shopify → data layer → GTM → GA4 / Google Ads / Meta. - Deliver a concise tracking plan (doc or sheet). 3) Implementation - Configure GTM tags, triggers, variables for all required events. - Fix/implement GA4 events and conversions, with working e‑commerce reporting. - Implement/fix Google Ads conversions and remarketing. - Implement/fix Meta pixel events and Conversions API with proper deduplication. 4) Testing & validation - Test full flow: landing → product → cart → checkout → purchase. - Validate events in GTM preview, GA4 DebugView, Google Ads, and Meta Events Manager. - Deliver a short validation summary plus a brief Loom or screenshots. 5) Handover - Provide GTM export/backup. - Short documentation or Loom explaining structure and how to add/change events. - 30–60 min handover call. Requirements Must have: - Strong experience with GTM, GA4 e‑commerce, Google Ads conversions, and Meta pixel/CAPI. - Proven Shopify tracking work (please show examples). - Ability to clean up a messy setup and deliver stable, validated tracking. - Clear communication. Nice to have: - Server‑side GTM, consent/cookie banner integrations, high‑AOV/medical‑adjacent e‑commerce. Proposal Instructions Please include: 1. 1–2 concrete project examples (Shopify or similar) with GTM + GA4 + Google Ads + Meta. 2. Your high‑level process (audit → plan → implementation → testing). 3. Confirmation of Shopify experience. 4. Estimated timeline for: - Audit + plan - Implementation + testing 5. Your price (one fixed fee preferred, or a clear breakdown). Project Details - Type: One‑time project with possible ongoing tracking tasks. - Start: ASAP. - Target timeline: - Audit + tracking plan: 5–7 days from hire. - Implementation + testing: within 2 weeks total.
jobs.title: Senior GTM / GA4 / Google Ads & Meta Tracking Specialist for Shopify
